starry_unigraph.runtime.flare.state module

RNN state management for multi-snapshot Flare training.

Provides RNNStateManager (per-snapshot hidden state storage with padding / mixing across a sliding window) and STGraphBlob (a sequence of DGL graphs with attached state management methods).

class starry_unigraph.runtime.flare.state.RNNStateManager(ends_list: list[int | None], mode: str = 'pad', disable_routes: bool = False)[source]

Bases: object

Per-snapshot RNN state storage with padding / mixing support.

Manages a sliding window of DGL graphs. Each graph is monkey-patched with flare_fetch_state(h) and flare_store_state(h) methods so that models can transparently read/write per-snapshot hidden state without knowing the underlying state management strategy.

Two modes are supported:

  • pad — always zero-pad state to the required number of nodes (used during chunk-decay warmup or eval).

  • mix — merge current state with a detached copy of the previous state (used during multi-frame training with overlapping windows).

Args:
ends_list: List of truncation endpoints (one per window slot).

None means “full snapshot, no truncation”.

mode: "pad" or "mix" — state initialization strategy. disable_routes: If True, remove routing info from added graphs.

Example:

states = RNNStateManager(ends_list=[None], mode="pad")
states.add(graph)
blob = STGraphBlob(states)
# model.forward(blob) will call graph.flare_fetch_state / flare_store_state
add(g: Any) → None[source]

Add a new graph to the sliding window.

Evicts the oldest graph if the window is full, then patches all graphs with state fetch/store methods bound to this manager.

Args:

g: A DGL graph (block or plain) for the next snapshot.

classmethod apply_state(fn, state: Any, old_state: Any | None = None) → Any[source]
classmethod patch_dummy_methods(g: Any) → Any[source]

Attach no-op flare_fetch_state / flare_store_state to a graph.

Used for single-graph eval/predict where no state manager exists.

Args:

g: A DGL graph or block.

Returns:

The same graph g with dummy methods attached in-place.

classmethod state_detach(state: Any) → Any[source]
classmethod state_mixing(state: Any, old_state: Any, end: int) → Any[source]

Mix current state with a previous state to fill end rows.

Rows [0, cur_size) come from state; remaining rows [cur_size, end) come from old_state (detached).

Args:

state: Current hidden state tensors. old_state: Previous hidden state tensors (same structure). end: Target number of rows.

Returns:

Mixed state with exactly end rows per tensor.

classmethod state_padding(state: Any, end: int) → Any[source]

Pad or truncate every tensor in state to end rows.

Args:
state: Nested structure (tensor, tuple, list, dict) of hidden

state tensors.

end: Target number of rows for dim-0.

Returns:

Same nested structure with all tensors resized to end rows.

class starry_unigraph.runtime.flare.state.STGraphBlob(state: RNNStateManager)[source]

Bases: object

Sequence of DGL graphs with attached RNN state management.

In training, backed by RNNStateManager (multi-frame window, state padding/mixing). In eval/predict, typically wraps a single graph with dummy state methods.

The blob is iterable and supports len() and __getitem__. The most recent graph is accessible via current_graph.

Args:

state: The RNNStateManager that owns the graph window.

Example:

blob = STGraphBlob(states)
for graph in blob:          # iterate over window frames
    x = graph.dstdata["x"]
last = blob.current_graph   # most recent snapshot
property current_graph: Any
describe() → dict[str, Any][source]
property flare_is_full_snapshot: bool
property snapshot_index: int
class starry_unigraph.runtime.flare.state.STWindowState(full_snapshot_limit: int = 1)[source]

Bases: object

Lightweight metadata tracker (not used in training path).

describe() → dict[str, Any][source]
